summary: Policy-backed control for cloud consumer account state content in Windows experiences. The local Microsoft CloudContent.admx file explicitly documents the same policy key, value name, and enabled/disabled values that the app uses.

setting.name: Cloud Consumer Account State Content
setting.casual_explanation: Some Windows experiences can use cloud-backed consumer account state content. This policy decides whether those experiences may use that content or must fall back to default content instead.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to reduce cloud-driven content in Windows experiences, simplify the user experience, or keep managed systems away from consumer-style content flows.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces cloud consumer content, but it can also make some Windows experiences less personalized or less dynamic.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents DisableConsumerAccountStateContent directly. CloudContent.admx maps it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent\DisableConsumerAccountStateContent with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the cloud consumer account content policy.

allowed_values.label: Cloud consumer content allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Windows experiences may use cloud consumer account state content.

allowed_values.label: Cloud consumer content disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows experiences using this client component fall back to default content instead.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableConsumerAccountStateContent = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 11 version 21H2 and newer supported systems

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents behavior for supported Enterprise-oriented editions; this record describes the policy baseline, not every UI surface affected by cloud content.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that should keep ordinary cloud-backed Windows experience content
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows experiences to keep their normal cloud consumer account content behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want those experiences to fall back to default content instead.
tradeoffs: Windows can continue using cloud consumer account state content where supported.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for this content path.

recommended_profiles.label: Managed minimal
avoid_for: Users who want the most dynamic Microsoft-provided experience content

states.rationale: Force Windows experiences using this component to use default fallback content.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want fewer cloud consumer content paths active in Windows experiences.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the related Windows experiences to stay as personalized and dynamic as possible.
tradeoffs: Some Microsoft-delivered experience content is reduced.
Affected Windows experiences may feel less dynamic or less personalized.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: DisableConsumerAccountStateContent
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that turns off cloud consumer account state content in Windows experiences.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableConsumerAccountStateContent with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the CloudContent policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableConsumerAccountStateContent = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft CloudContent.admx file explicitly defines DisableConsumerAccountStateContent at SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0, which matches the app's policy surface.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same policy key and value name that the app writes and confirms the 1/0 mapping.
